Hedger Pressure Indicator
← Back to dashboard

Hedger Pressure Indicator

CoT hedger pressure: supply expansion & shortage stress.

As of 2026-02-17

Gemini Summary

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement (mandatory): Given a Hedger_SupplyPressure_z of 0.866 and a deep-short share of 25.5%, this setup aligns with Downward-biased price paths and Normal volatility, where the dominant risk is Regime shift, not physical shortage.
  • The signal has transitioned into a Moderate Supply Expansion regime as commercial entities increase forward selling (1).
  • Conviction Band: Medium; Interpretation Confidence: High Confidence; Internal Conflict Flag: No.

Methodology Applied:

  • Supply Expansion Pressure is triggered when commercial hedgers are heavily short, with z-scores ≤ -1.0 per market (1).
  • The "Moderate" regime classification is applied when the aggregate robust z-score of short shares exceeds 0.75 (1).
  • High z-scores for short positioning are interpreted as expectations of increased production or weaker forward prices (1).
  • Dataset: Unified enriched CoT dataset, latest observation 2026-02-17.

Key Dynamics:

  • The primary driver is the rise in Hedger_SupplyPressure_z to 0.866, confirming a breach of the moderate threshold (1).
  • Momentum has accelerated over the last three reports, with the deep-short share rising from 20.7% to 25.5%.
  • Physical demand remains neutral, as indicated by the Normal Shortage/Stress regime z-score of -0.359.
  • Conditional Invalidation: Hedger_SupplyPressure_z falling below 0.75.

Scenario Balance:

  • Dominant base case: Continued Moderate Supply Expansion as producers lock in prices, suggesting a cap on rallies.
  • Most plausible upside risk: A shift back to "Normal" if commercial short-covering occurs during supply disruptions.
  • Most plausible downside risk: Acceleration to "High Supply Expansion" if the robust z-score exceeds 1.5.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Tactical (weeks), as CoT shifts guide short-term fundamental positioning shifts (1).
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Medium, given the clear trend and lack of internal conflicts.

Macro Relevance:

  • Informs the Global Trade & Demand dimension by highlighting potential oversupply or producer bearishness.
  • Cycle position: Mid-to-late expansion, where increased production begins to outweigh marginal demand.
  • Typically serves as a cross-check to identify when speculative rallies lack physical market support (1).

Data & References:

  • Unified enriched CoT dataset; latest observation: 2026-02-17.
  • Influential datapoints: Hedger_SupplyPressure_z (0.866) and hedger_deep_short_share (0.255).
  • LME inventory data and global freight rates would provide additional physical validation.
